rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Peek at next module before adding a second exercise
Between-module reading appears in two places
Cross-module artefact contracts explicit at both ends
Session boundaries and working directories are designed, not implicit
rules_evaluated.evidence: M1 `## Next` (line 49): names what M2 covers but includes no close instruction (`/clear`, end-of-module wrap, or 'kill this session'). M2 `## Prework` (lines 6–12): names the prework task but does not name the working directory (no 'open a new Claude Code session in `<your-repo-path>`' or equivalent). Both the close mechanic and the open working-directory are implicit.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a one-sentence close to M1 `## Next` (e.g., '`/clear` after the ticket closes — M2 opens fresh in the same repo.') and add a one-sentence open to M2 `## Prework` naming the working directory explicitly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts — contract row in producing module maintainer block
Cross-module structural-failure rescue callouts when prior send-off can fail
Audit-discipline — walk M(N-1) close before flagging M(N) as spec drift
Bring-or-scramble plain-stakes-line shape at module homework boundaries
rules_evaluated.evidence: M2 `## Prework` (lines 6–10): required prework ('Surface one multi-file backlog task in conversation with Claude') has no bring-or-scramble stakes line at the M1 close or M2 open. M1 `## Homework after Module 1` (line 53) explicitly says 'Skipping either piece does not break Module 2' — correctly optional, no stakes cost — but the required M2 prework task has no corresponding stakes line at the boundary. Canonical form absent: 'Come to Module 2 without a surfaced multi-file task and you'll be scrambling to find one while the room is already in plan mode. Your call.'
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add one bring-or-scramble stakes sentence to M1 `## Next` or M2 `## Prework` open for the required prework item (surfacing the multi-file task), in the canonical form: 'Come to Module 2 without X and you'll be scrambling for Y while the room is already Z. Your call.'
